assumptions: Architecture (extend the existing domain-analysis snapshot to pre-compute the agreement matrix vs. introduce a dedicated agreement-snapshot system in the Win Rate stability style) is a plan-stage decision. The spec captures the user-visible goal regardless of approach.
Cache key includes scope + signature + domain(s) + the model-set so different selections do not collide.
The existing 'Preparing model agreement report' UI stays as the cold-build path. Hot path (cached) returns immediately with no Preparing state.
A 'Cached as of …' freshness chip on /models follows the existing cluster-cache chip pattern in the Domain Analysis cache.

Cache is invalidated/refreshed when new completed runs change the underlying data for that scope; the resolver returns the cached snapshot and queues a refresh on miss or stale, mirroring the Win Rate stability pattern.
V1 (/models) and V2 (/models/v2) both benefit equally; no split logic — the cache lives below the V1/V2 split.
discovery.summary: Make the Model Agreement on Value Tradeoffs report load near-instantly on /models and /models/v2 by giving its data the same kind of server-side snapshot caching the Win Rate Stability report just got. Today the modelAgreementOnTradeoffs resolver already has snapshot-read and refresh-queue plumbing (piggy-backing on the domain-analysis snapshot), but heavy scopes still stay on 'Preparing model agreement report' for minutes. Four things on the page depend on this single computation — the agreement section, the similarity table, the kappa-clustering dendrogram, and the V2 internal-agreement overlay — so caching it once makes all four instant.
non_goals: No changes to the kappa math or pairwise-comparison logic itself.
No UI redesign on /models or /models/v2 beyond the freshness chip.
No backfill scripts beyond what the snapshot builder fills in over time as it runs.
No changes to the V1/V2 page structure or routing.
No changes to ModelAgreementSection's existing 'Preparing…' UI — that stays as the cold-build state.
acceptance_criteria: On a selection whose snapshot already exists, the Model Agreement on Value Tradeoffs table renders in well under one second after the GraphQL response (not minutes).

On a fresh selection with no cached snapshot, the resolver returns pending and queues a refresh; the existing 'Preparing model agreement report' UI shows; subsequent loads of the same selection are near-instant.
The /models page shows a 'Cached as of …' freshness indicator (chip/timestamp) on the agreement-driven reports, matching the existing cluster-cache chip pattern.
Different selections (different scope / signature / domain(s) / model-set) get independent cache entries with no collisions — switching domains or signatures does not serve the wrong report.
When new completed runs change the underlying data, the snapshot is invalidated/refreshed so users do not see stale numbers indefinitely.

All four agreement-derived surfaces on /models become instant when the snapshot exists: the agreement section, the similarity table, the kappa-clustering dendrogram in kappa-agreement mode, and the V2 internal-agreement overlay.
API tests cover cache hit, cache miss (queues refresh, returns pending), and stale-then-refresh paths.
Web lint/test/build and API lint/test/build all pass; no @ts-ignore, no any.
No regression to V1's rendered output on /models — existing tests still pass unchanged.

parallel_analysis.note: No parallel opportunities. Slice 2 depends on slice 1's computeModelAgreement, canonicalKey, and computeInputFingerprint. Slice 3 depends on slice 2's snapshot cache. Slice 4 depends on slice 3's GraphQL freshness fields. Strict pipeline.
